mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a distribution that provides a robust platform for deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters in enterprise environments. It leverages the Tanzu Framework and Carvel packaging to offer an integrated experience for users. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with VMware's ecosystem.

steps.title: Install the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Tanzu CLI, which is essential for managing Tanzu Kubernetes Grid.

```bash

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/tanzu

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> Download the Tanzu CLI binary.
> Make the Tanzu CLI executable.

steps.title: Initialize the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step initializes the Tanzu CLI and sets up the necessary configurations.

```bash

tanzu init

```

> Initialize the Tanzu CLI with default settings.

steps.title: Create a Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Tanzu with specified version and node count.

```bash

tanzu cluster create my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --plan dev --num-nodes 3

```

> Create a cluster named 'my-cluster' with 3 nodes.
commands: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --plan dev --num-nodes 3

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context

```

> Get the kubeconfig for the admin user of 'my-cluster'.

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

tanzu cluster delete my-cluster

```

> This will delete the cluster 'my-cluster' and all associated resources.

uninstall.title: Remove the Tanzu CLI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Tanzu CLI from your system.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> This will remove the Tanzu CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, upgrade the Kubernetes version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

tanzu cluster backup my-cluster

tanzu cluster upgrade my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup the current state of the cluster.
> Upgrade the cluster to Kubernetes version 1.29.1.
> Verify that the nodes are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster creation failed due to insufficient resources in the environment.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your environment meets the minimum resource requirements (2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeconfig context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig context for the specified cluster does not exist.

**Fix:**
Run 'tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context' to set the correct context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster is still initializing or has encountered an issue.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the cluster with 'tanzu cluster get my-cluster' and troubleshoot based on the output.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl CLI is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the official installation guide or ensure it's in your PATH.

versionNotes.changes: Support for Kubernetes 1.29, improved cluster lifecycle management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure to backup your clusters and follow the upgrade procedure.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with the status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --plan dev --num-nodes 3

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
